title: Puerto Banus Boat Rental: Costs, Seasons and Tips for 2026
html:
Puerto Banus remains one of Europe's most glamorous boating destinations. From the exclusive marina, you can charter a motor yacht from around 2,000 EUR for a half-day or a small speedboat from 350 EUR. BoatHire24 offers a secure marketplace to compare and book a boat that suits your budget.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

You can depart from Puerto Banus Marina, with several other nearby harbours offering alternative launch points. The main marina is the hub for most charters, but you can also set off from Estepona, Marbella, San Pedro de Alcántara or the smaller docks in Cabopino. Each location gives you a different angle on the Costa del Sol coastline. A trip from Puerto Banus to Marbella's coast takes about 20 minutes by motorboat.

Which boat to rent

Choosing the right boat depends on your group size and how you want to spend the day. Here is a list of popular types available on BoatHire24:

  1. Motor yacht (30, 40ft), ideal for luxury cruising with up to 12 guests; from 1,500 EUR for a half-day.
  2. Rib or speedboat, perfect for thrill-seekers and small groups of 4, 6; from 350 EUR for two hours.
  3. Catamaran, stable and spacious for 12, 14 people, with deck space for lounging; from 2,500 EUR full-day.
  4. Fishing boat, equipped for deep-sea fishing off the coast; from 600 EUR for a half-day.
  5. Luxury superyacht, crewed, for up to 50 guests, with full catering; from 10,000 EUR for a full-day charter.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

Prices range from 350 EUR for a shared tour to over 10,000 EUR for a full-day superyacht charter. Here is a breakdown of typical price bands for 2026:

  • Shared tour on a speedboat: from 350 EUR per person.
  • Small motorboat (half-day, self-drive): from 400 EUR.
  • Catamaran (full-day, skipper included): from 3,000 EUR.
  • Luxury crewed yacht (full-day): from 7,000 EUR.

For the best selection of boats, consider yacht charter Puerto Banus options on BoatHire24, where you can filter by price and type.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

Licence requirements depend on the boat's power and length, but the operator will confirm exactly what you need. In Spain, a recreational licence is required for most private powerboats over 5 metres or with engines above 15 horsepower. However, many charter boats include a skipper, so you can simply relax. The high season runs from June to September, when the Levante wind can pick up in the afternoon, always check the forecast with your skipper. Here is a genuine local tip:

If you book in July, plan to depart by 10am to avoid the strongest afternoon sea breezes and secure a prime spot at the marinas.

At a glance

  • Price band: 350, 10,000+ EUR
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Maximum capacity: up to 50 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Puerto Banus?

Yes, many boats come with a skipper, so no personal licence is needed for crewed charters.

What is the best time to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

The best weather and sea conditions are from May to October, with July and August being the busiest and most expensive.

What marine life can I see while boating from Puerto Banus?

Dolphins are frequently spotted along the coast, especially near the Cabo de Gata area to the east.

How far is it to Gibraltar by boat from Puerto Banus?

Gibraltar is about 45 nautical miles south-west, a journey of roughly two hours at cruising speed.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own. For a direct option, browse the Mefasa 90 listing if you are seeking a high-performance motor yacht. Whether you want to rent or become a host, BoatHire24 makes it simple to find or offer a boat.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
